display.purpose: Hero headline only; Hanken Grotesk at the clamp ceiling.

body.purpose: Reading copy in Literata, capped at 65-75ch.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: Floating surfaces only: dialog, sheet, popover, menu, the control pills.

shadows.name: accent-glow
shadows.purpose: Hero CTA and 2056-mode surfaces; a glow, not a drop shadow.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: The standard easing for reveals, presses, and transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The main call to action; Cray Blue fill with near-white label.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 3rem; padding: 0 1.5rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.625rem; background: oklch(0.58 0.11 232); color: oklch(0.99 0.004 240); font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.2s, transform 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.52 0.11 232); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.58 0.11 232); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action; bordered and transparent with ink text.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 3rem; padding: 0 1.5rem; border: 1px solid oklch(0.91 0.008 240); border-radius: 0.625rem; background: transparent; color: oklch(0.25 0.02 245); font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 1rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.975 0.005 240); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.58 0.11 232); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Input
components.description: Single-line text field; hairline border, surface fill, focus-ring on focus.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; height: 2.5rem; padding: 0 0.75rem; border: 1px solid oklch(0.91 0.008 240); border-radius: 0.625rem; background: oklch(0.99 0.004 240); color: oklch(0.25 0.02 245); font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; outline: none; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.52 0.02 245); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(0.58 0.11 232); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat surface container; border and tone separate it, no resting shadow.
components.html:
Network management

Proactive monitoring and maintenance, so problems get fixed before you notice them.
components.css: .ds-card { border: 1px solid oklch(0.91 0.008 240); border-radius: 1rem; background: oklch(0.975 0.005 240); padding: 1.5rem; transition: transform 0.2s, border-color 0.2s; } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); border-color: oklch(0.58 0.11 232 / 0.4); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.125rem; color: oklch(0.25 0.02 245); } .ds-card-body { margin: 0.5rem 0 0; font-family: 'Literata Variable', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.6; color: oklch(0.52 0.02 245); }

components.name: Primary Badge
components.description: Soft-tinted pill; the strong blue text tier keeps colored text >= 4.5:1.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 1.5rem; padding: 0.125rem 0.625rem; background: oklch(0.58 0.11 232 / 0.1); color: oklch(0.46 0.11 232); font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.8125rem; }

narrative.overview: Cray Networks is the local IT expert who treats you like a neighbor: warm and plain-spoken, yet engineered with the precision of serious tooling. The system reads as dependable infrastructure with a human face, built on continuity, the real Cray blue-and-white identity finally systematized in OKLCH with a first-class dark mode. Typography carries the brand: a Hanken Grotesk display against a Literata reading face. The medium is the proof; the token system, instant live theming, and accessibility are demonstrated, not claimed.
keyCharacteristics: Warm but precise: neighborly voice, Linear-grade restraint and taut spacing.
Typography-forward: a genuine serif/sans pairing on real variable-font axes.
OKLCH-native: every color is a composable channel, so the whole UI re-themes live.
Continuity over reinvention: enhance the real Cray blue-and-white, do not replace it.
Accessibility engineered: AA verified with APCA, light and dark, reduced-motion honored.

rules.name: The One Amber Rule
rules.body: Amber appears on at most ~5% of any screen. Its rarity is the point; it is never a second brand color.

rules.name: The Continuity Rule
rules.body: The blue is Cray's existing blue, refined, not replaced. Warmth comes from amber, typography, and copy, never a new hue family.

rules.name: The Serif-Reads Rule
rules.body: Reading and reassurance copy is set in Literata, never the sans. The serif is the warmth signal.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Cards and panels are flat; they separate by border and surface tone. A shadow means the element floats above the page.

dos: Do author every color in OKLCH as composable channels; keep light and dark on the same channel.
Do set reading copy in Literata and UI/labels in Hanken Grotesk; cap body measure at 65-75ch.
Do verify contrast (AA, APCA) in both themes; use the *-strong text tiers for colored text on light tints.
Do keep amber to ~5% of a screen.
Do ship light and dark and honor prefers-reduced-motion on every animation.
donts: Don't use stock data-center photos, clip-art icons, or corporate gradient blues.
Don't fall back to the corporate-blue SaaS / bootstrap template look.
Don't ship AI-slop tells: cream/sand backgrounds, per-section uppercase eyebrows, 01/02/03 markers, identical card grids, gradient text.
Don't drift toward cold enterprise-dashboard sterility; warmth is mandatory.
Don't add a colored border-left/right stripe, a resting card drop-shadow, or a second brand hue.
